items.headline: Major commercial/industrial project approved on West San Carlos
items.summary: A $696,570 commercial/industrial development is underway at 1300 W San Carlos St, marking significant economic activity in San José's commercial corridor. This is the largest permitted project in this batch and suggests continued business investment in the area.

items.headline: City commissions outline priorities for coming year
items.summary: Saratoga's five commissions—covering parks, planning, traffic safety, heritage preservation, and library/public art—presented their 2026-27 work plans to the City Council. Residents interested in upcoming projects affecting parks, development, traffic concerns, or cultural initiatives may want to track these commission meetings.

items.headline: Cupertino launches business license amnesty program for shopping centers
items.summary: The city is offering a business license amnesty program targeted at shopping centers, part of a broader push to support retail and small businesses in Cupertino. This could help previously non-compliant businesses get licensed without penalties while boosting local commerce.

items.headline: Great American Litter Pick-up becomes annual city-sponsored event
items.summary: The City Council approved the Great American Litter Pick-up as an official annual city-sponsored special event on March 25, 2026. The city will now dedicate funds and can accept donations for this community cleanup initiative.

items.headline: City seeks input on federal housing grant funding
items.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission reviewed applications for Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) funding on March 25. These federal funds support affordable housing and community development projects that directly impact local residents.

items.headline: Palo Alto considers new retail vitality policies and zoning changes
items.summary: The Planning Commission is reviewing an ordinance to amend zoning and building regulations aimed at boosting retail activity across the city. The changes implement policies from Palo Alto's Comprehensive Plan and could affect where retail businesses can operate and how they develop.

items.headline: San Antonio Road area could see major land use and transportation changes
items.summary: The Planning Commission held a study session on alternatives for the San Antonio Road area plan, examining different approaches to land use and transportation. This could reshape how that corridor develops and functions for residents and commuters.

items.headline: Campbell updates beekeeping rules with new ordinance
items.summary: The city council adopted an updated beekeeping ordinance that will govern how residents can keep bees on their properties. The changes modernize regulations that affect anyone interested in urban beekeeping in Campbell.
